channel.title: EDGAR Live Scanner
channel.description: High-signal SEC EDGAR filings — tender offers, going-private, activist stakes, spinoffs, delistings, busted deals.

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · INVE
item.ns0:headline: Sells IoT Business to Trackonomy for $50M in preferred stock; vote pending
item.description:
Sells IoT Business to Trackonomy for $50M in preferred stock; vote pending

Sells its specialty IoT business to Trackonomy Systems for $50M of Series C Preferred Stock (valued at $20.07/share) plus assumption of related liabilities. The company will continue as a public entity focused on physical AI solutions via targeted SaaS acquisitions. The transaction was approved by the board and requires stockholder approval; Bleichroeder holders (~12% of common, 100% of Series B preferred) have agreed to vote in favor.

About: Identiv, Inc., develops, manufactures, and supplies specialty IoT products in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, and the Asia-Pacific. The company offers specialty radio-frequency identification (RFID) and Internet of Things (IoT) devices, including near field communication, high frequency, dual frequency, ultra-high frequency, and bluetooth low energy that are attached to or embedded into physical items, such as medical device consumables, pill containers, wine bottles, consumer applia…

  • Counterparty: Trackonomy Systems, Inc.
  • Deal value: $50,000,000
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-06-24
  • Next catalyst: Stockholder vote at annual meeting

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · CABR
item.ns0:headline: Forced Nasdaq delisting determination; hearing requested by July 22
item.description:
Forced Nasdaq delisting determination; hearing requested by July 22

Received a Staff Delisting Determination from Nasdaq denying its request for continued listing due to non-compliance with equity requirements. The company intends to request a hearing by July 21, which will stay the suspension pending the Panel's decision. If the hearing is not requested, trading will be suspended on July 24.

About: Caring Brands, Inc., a wellness consumer products company, provides over-the-counter and cosmetic consumer products. It offers Hair Enzyme Booster (JW-700) for the treatment of hair growth; Photocil for the treatment of psoriasis and vitiligo; and CB-101 (Eczema Treatment) for atopic dermatitis (eczema).

  • Announced: 2026-07-15
  • Next catalyst: Hearing request deadline July 22, 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · HWBK
item.ns0:headline: Acquires FSC Bancshares for $14M cash + 413K HWBK shares; vote Sept 1
item.description:
Acquires FSC Bancshares for $14M cash + 413K HWBK shares; vote Sept 1

Acquires FSC Bancshares (FBI) in a cash-and-stock merger valued at approximately $28.3M. FBI shareholders will receive a combination of $14M aggregate cash and 413,101 HWBK shares, subject to adjustment based on FBI's tangible equity. The FBI special meeting to vote on the merger is scheduled for September 1, 2026.

About: Hawthorn Bancshares, Inc. operates as the bank holding company for Hawthorn Bank that provides various banking products and services to families and businesses. It offers checking and savings accounts, and certificates of deposit. The company also provides commercial and industrial, single payment personal, mortgage, installment, and commercial and residential real estate loans.

  • Counterparty: FSC Bancshares, Inc.
  • Deal value: $28.3M
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Announced: 2026-04-29
  • Next catalyst: FBI special meeting on September 1, 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · BFC
item.ns0:headline: Acquires PSB Holdings for 0.3470 BFC shares per PSB share; vote pending
item.description:
Acquires PSB Holdings for 0.3470 BFC shares per PSB share; vote pending

Will acquire PSB Holdings, Inc. (PSBQ) in a stock-for-stock merger. Each PSB share will be converted into 0.3470 shares of BFC common stock. The deal was announced on May 19, 2026, and PSB shareholders will vote at a special meeting. The merger is subject to regulatory approvals and other conditions. At announcement, the implied value was approximately $49.85 per PSB share, an 80.6% premium over PSB's last closing price.

About: Bank First Corporation operates as a holding company for Bank First, N.A. that provides consumer and commercial financial services to businesses, professionals, and consumers, in Wisconsin. It offers checking, savings, money market, cash management, retirement, and health savings accounts; other time deposits; certificates of deposit; and residential mortgage products.

  • Counterparty: PSB Holdings, Inc.
  • Offer price: 49.85
  • Premium: 80.6
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-19
  • Next catalyst: PSB special meeting vote

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · AA
item.ns0:headline: Acquires South32's AliGroup assets for $3.1B cash + $1B stock + CVR
item.description:
Acquires South32's AliGroup assets for $3.1B cash + $1B stock + CVR

Announced the strategic acquisition of South32's bauxite, alumina, and aluminum assets (AliGroup) for $3.1 billion cash and $1 billion in stock, plus a contingent value right capped at $750 million. The deal includes a locked-box mechanism estimated at over $200M and a ticking fee of ~$80-100M. Alcoa expects $900M NPV synergies and accretion to EPS and cash flow immediately after close, with South32 shareholder vote expected in Oct/Nov 2026.

About: Alcoa Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the bauxite mining, alumina refining, aluminum production, and energy generation business in Australia, Brazil, Canada, Iceland, Norway, Spain, the United States, and internationally. The company operates through two segments: Alumina and Aluminum.

  • Counterparty: South32 Limited
  • Deal value: $4.1B (cash+stock) + up to $750M CVR
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Expected Close: Q4 2026 (after South32 shareholder vote)
  • Next catalyst: South32 shareholder approval in Oct/Nov 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · TMRC
item.ns0:headline: Acquired by USA Rare Earth for 3.8M shares; proxy supplemented
item.description:
Acquired by USA Rare Earth for 3.8M shares; proxy supplemented

Is being acquired by USA Rare Earth, Inc. (USAR) in a stock-for-stock merger. Each TMRC share will be converted into a pro-rata portion of 3,823,328 USAR shares. The definitive proxy statement has been supplemented with additional financial projections and details in response to shareholder lawsuits. The merger is subject to shareholder approval and other customary conditions.

About: Texas Mineral Resources Corp. acquires, explores, and develops mineral properties in the United States. Its flagship project is the Round Top rare earths project covering an area of 950 acres and prospecting permits covering 9,345 acres located in Hudspeth County, Texas. The company was formerly known as Texas Rare Earth Resources Corp. and changed its name to Texas Mineral Resources Corp. in March 2016. Texas Mineral Resources Corp.

  • Counterparty: USA Rare Earth, Inc.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-03-04

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · USAR
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Texas Mineral Resources for 3.8M USAR shares; lawsuits filed
item.description:
Acquires Texas Mineral Resources for 3.8M USAR shares; lawsuits filed

Is acquiring Texas Mineral Resources Corp. (TMRC) in a stock-for-stock merger. TMRC stockholders will receive an aggregate of 3,823,328 shares of USAR common stock. Two stockholder lawsuits have been filed challenging the transaction, and USAR and TMRC have issued supplemental disclosures to address the claims.

About: USA Rare Earth, Inc. engages in mining, processing, and supplying rare earths and other critical minerals in the United States, Europe, and Asia. It primarily explores neodymium, dysprosium, terbium, yttrium, gallium, hafnium, praseodymium, samarium, and other critical minerals. The company holds interests in the Round Top Mountain located near Sierra Blanca, Texas.

  • Counterparty: Texas Mineral Resources Corp.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-03-04
  • Next catalyst: Stockholder vote pending; lawsuits filed July 7-8, 2026

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Lost · NAAS
item.ns0:headline: Loses arbitration award over terminated Charge Amps acquisition; reviewing
item.description:
Loses arbitration award over terminated Charge Amps acquisition; reviewing

Received a final arbitration award against it and its subsidiary Fleetin AB, finding breach of a share purchase agreement for the proposed acquisition of Charge Amps AB (terminated Nov 2023). The tribunal awarded damages, statutory interest, and legal fees to the four seller claimants. The company is reviewing the award and believes it does not affect ongoing operations.

About: NaaS Technology Inc. provides electric vehicle (EV) charging services in China. It offers online EV charging solutions to charging stations, including mobility connectivity services through Kuaidian; SaaS products, such as traffic support and management, marketing, payment, chargers management, order management, load management, and membership management; and operates full station.

Amends GOWell merger: earnout at 80% EBITDA, expense cap $9M

Amended its business combination agreement with GOWell Technology Limited. The amendment allows partial earnout at 80% achievement of 2026 EBITDA target (previously only at 90%) and increases the cap on SPAC transaction expenses from $8M to $9M. The deal was originally announced in October 2025.

About: Inflection Point Acquisition Corp. V does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, amalgamation, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses or entities. The company was formerly known as Maywood Acquisition Corp. and changed its name to Inflection Point Acquisition Corp. V in November 2025. The company was incorporated in 2024 and is based in New York, New York.

item.title: 💧 Capital Return · Recap · GIPR
item.ns0:headline: Amends preferred unit terms to allow 1:1 common conversion; aims to regain Nasdaq listing
item.description:
Amends preferred unit terms to allow 1:1 common conversion; aims to regain Nasdaq listing

Entered into amendments with holders of its Series B-1 and B-2 preferred units. The holders waived cash redemption rights and can exchange units for common stock at 1:1 (post-reverse split). The preferred return on B-2 units increased from $0.33 to $0.39 per unit. The company believes the reclassification as permanent equity will bring stockholders' equity above $2.5M, potentially regaining Nasdaq compliance. The delisting extension runs to August 4, 2026.

About: Generation Income Properties, Inc., located in Tampa, Florida, is an internally managed real estate investment trust formed to acquire and own, directly and jointly, real estate investments focused on retail, office, and industrial net lease properties in densely populated submarkets. Generation Income Properties, Inc. was established on September 19, 2015 and incorporated in Maryland, USA.

  • Counterparty: LMB Owenton I LLC and Lloyd M. Bernstein
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-07-16
  • Next catalyst: Nasdaq panel decision on compliance; next periodic report

Settled potential False Claims Act claims with the DOJ and OIG-HHS for $4,678,981.86 plus interest, resolving allegations related to DEXYCU sales and marketing practices. The company also entered a five-year Corporate Integrity Agreement requiring compliance oversight. Payment is due within 14 days; the company expects to use cash on hand.

The company's pipeline leverages its proprietary bioerodible Durasert E technology for sustained intraocular drug delivery. item.title: 👀 Activist · EVGN
item.ns0:headline: Faces activist campaign demanding removal of all directors except CEO
item.description:
Faces activist campaign demanding removal of all directors except CEO

Disclosed that certain shareholders filed a Schedule 13D on July 10, 2026, demanding an extraordinary general meeting to remove all current board members except the CEO and replace them with activist nominees. The company updated its risk factors to reflect the potential impact of the activist campaign, including diversion of resources, uncertainty, and possible governance instability. The outcome of the demand and any resulting shareholder vote remains uncertain.

About: Evogene Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a computational biology company in Israel, the United States, Europe, and Africa. It operates through three segments: Agriculture, Human Health, and Industrial Applications. The company engages in the agricultural activities, including seed traits and ag-chemicals activities; and design novel small molecules for drug development by utilizing ChemPass AI, a computational generative AI engine.

  • Counterparty: Activist shareholders (unnamed in filing)
  • Announced: 2026-07-10
  • Next catalyst: Extraordinary general meeting date (not yet set)

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · DXLG
item.ns0:headline: Board withdraws support for FBB merger; recommends AGAINST issuance proposal
item.description:
Board withdraws support for FBB merger; recommends AGAINST issuance proposal

Destination XL Group's board has withdrawn its prior recommendation for the merger with FBB Holdings I, Inc., and now recommends voting AGAINST the issuance proposal. The board cites concerns about FBB's debt, potential negative equity, and substantial dilution to DXL stockholders—FBB stockholders would own 55% of the combined company. The merger agreement remains in effect; FBB can terminate and collect a $2.5M termination fee. A special meeting vote is pending.

About: Destination XL Group, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a specialty retailer of big and tall men’s clothing and footwear in the United States. The company’s stores offer sportswear and dresswear; fashion-neutral items, including jeans, casual pants, t-shirts, polo shirts, dress shirts, and suit separates; and casual clothing. It also provides vintage-screen t-shirts and wovens under various private labels.

  • Counterparty: FBB Holdings I, Inc.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2025-12-11
  • Next catalyst: Special meeting vote on merger

Px $0.6200 · Mkt cap $34.4M · EV $231.3M · Fwd EV/EBITDA 65.3x · Consumer Discretionary / Specialty Retail

item.title: 🤝 Merger · Definitive · WFRD
item.ns0:headline: Acquires NCS Multistage for 0.5537 WFRD shares or mixed election
item.description:
Acquires NCS Multistage for 0.5537 WFRD shares or mixed election

To acquire NCS Multistage Holdings, Inc. (NCSM) in a stock/cash election merger. NCS stockholders may elect to receive 0.5537 WFRD shares per NCS share (Share Consideration) or a mixed consideration of cash equal to 0.1371 WFRD shares' value plus 0.2392 WFRD shares (Mixed Consideration), subject to proration. The implied value per NCS share was $57.39 (all share election) based on WFRD closing price of $103.64 on May 29, 2026. The majority stockholder (Advent, ~56.33%) has already consented, so no stockholder vote is required. The transaction is expected to close upon satisfaction of regulatory and other conditions.

About: Weatherford International plc, an energy services company, provides equipment and services for the drilling, evaluation, completion, production, and intervention of oil, geothermal, and natural gas wells worldwide. The company operates through three segments: Drilling and Evaluation; Well Construction and Completions; and Production and Intervention.

item.title: 🏦 Demutual. · RBKB
item.ns0:headline: Mutual-to-stock conversion oversubscribed; 1.3978x exchange; close July 21
item.description:
Mutual-to-stock conversion oversubscribed; 1.3978x exchange; close July 21

Announced results of its mutual-to-stock conversion subscription offering, which was oversubscribed in the first tier by eligible depositors. Existing shareholders will receive 1.3978 new shares for each old share, with cash in lieu of fractional shares at $10.00 per share. Closing is expected on July 21, 2026, and shares will continue trading as RBKB on Nasdaq.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · SNY
item.ns0:headline: FDA approves subcutaneous Sarclisa Escena for multiple myeloma
item.description:
FDA approves subcutaneous Sarclisa Escena for multiple myeloma

The FDA approved Sanofi's subcutaneous Sarclisa Escena (isatuximab-irfc) for multiple myeloma across all existing indications of the IV formulation. It is the first anticancer treatment administered via an on-body injector. Approval based on IRAKLIA phase 3 study demonstrating non-inferior efficacy and safety vs IV infusion.

About: Sanofi engages in the research, development, manufacture, and marketing of therapeutic solutions. It provides immunology and inflammation, rare diseases neurology, oncology, and other vaccines. It also offers poliomyelitis, pertussis, and haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) pediatric vaccines; respiratory syncytial virus protection and hexavalent combination vaccines that includes hepatitis A, typhoid, yellow fever, and rabies vaccines.

  • Announced: 2026-07-10
  • Next catalyst: FDA approval granted

item.description:
Acquired by GH Power in reverse merger; ~9% stake in combined co.

Entered a definitive business combination agreement with GH Power, a private Ontario company. Under the deal, GH Power and Matinas will become wholly owned subsidiaries of a newly formed Ontario corporation, GHP International. Existing Matinas stockholders will receive GHP International shares and are expected to own approximately 9% of the combined company, while GH Power shareholders will own ~91%. The transaction is subject to shareholder approvals, Ontario court approval, SEC registration, a minimum $15M PIPE financing, and NYSE American listing; expected close in Q4 2026.

About: Matinas BioPharma Holdings, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, identifies and develops pharmaceutical products using its lipid nanocrystal (LNC) platform technology. The company’s LNC delivery technology platform utilizes lipid nanocrystals for the delivery of small molecules, nucleic acids, gene therapies, vaccines, proteins, and peptides.

item.ns0:headline: Faces Goldenwise Capital activist campaign; rejects board seat demands
item.description:
Faces Goldenwise Capital activist campaign; rejects board seat demands

Issued a press release responding to Goldenwise Capital Group's amended Schedule 13D and open letter demanding board seats. The company rejected the demands, citing lack of a credible value-creation plan, factual errors, and impractical deadlines. Goldenwise holds a 6.9% stake acquired for ~$2.6M, while Phunware has over $90M in cash; the board reaffirmed its 2.0 strategy.

About: Phunware, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides integrated software platform that equips companies with the products, solutions, and services to engage, manage, and monetize their mobile application portfolios in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments: software subscriptions and services and advertising.

item.description:
Board reveals insider-led withhold campaign; SEC violations alleged

Corporation disclosed that its 2026 annual meeting vote results were affected by a stealth proxy campaign involving at least one director, leading to unusually high withhold votes for two nominees. The board concluded the campaign violated fiduciary duties and federal securities laws, but all five director nominees were re-elected under plurality voting. The company adjourned the meeting to investigate and plans to take further action.

item.description:

FDA approved REVTORPYK (gedatolisib) for HR+/HER2-, PIK3CA wild-type locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer after prior endocrine therapy. Phase 3 VIKTORIA-1 trial showed 76% and 67% reduction in risk of progression or death with REVTORPYK combinations vs fulvestrant. item.description:
Spin-off from Resideo on track for Aug 3; targets $6B revenue by 2030

Provided standalone strategy and medium-term financial targets at its inaugural investor day, ahead of its spin-off from Resideo Technologies. The spin-off is expected to complete on August 3, 2026, with ADI common stock trading on NYSE under ADIG starting August 4. Targets include 4-6% revenue CAGR to ~$6B by 2030, adjusted EBITDA margin >8% by 2030, and cumulative cash flow from operations >$1B by 2030.

About: As of March 30, 2026 Abrdn Diversified Income And Growth Plc fund has gone out of business. Abrdn Diversified Income And Growth Plc is a closed ended balanced mutual fund launched by BlackRock Fund Managers Limited. The fund is co-managed by BlackRock Investment Management (UK) Limited and Aberdeen Fund Managers Limited. It invests in public equity and fixed income markets across the globe. The fund seeks to invest in securities of companies operating across diversified sectors.

  • Counterparty: Resideo Technologies, Inc.

item.description:

Guardian Metal Resources PLC's board is evaluating strategic alternatives for its seven non-core exploration projects (White Elephant, Pilot North, Cinch, Garfield, Golconda, Stonewall, Kibby Basin) to concentrate resources on advancing the co-flagship Pilot Mountain and Tempiute tungsten projects toward commercial production. Options may include sale, joint venture, or spin-off. The company will update the market on material developments.

About: Guardian Metal Resources PLC engages in the exploration and exploitation of mineral resources in the United States. It primarily explores tungsten, copper, gold, silver, lithium, gallium, and zinc deposits. The company’s flagship project is the 100% owned Pilot Mountain Project that located in Nevada. It also holds an option to acquire up to 100% interest in the Tempiute Project, co-flagship property, located in Nevada.

item.description:

The Delaware Court approved the settlement on June 30, 2026, and the agreement was executed on July 8, 2026. The agreement also requires Pre-IPO Holders' approval for CEO and other executive compensation decisions.

item.description:

Co. entered into a definitive merger agreement with USFM Corporation, under which USFM Merger Sub will merge into Twin Vee, with Twin Vee surviving as a wholly-owned subsidiary of USFM. Twin Vee shareholders will receive a pro rata portion of 10% of USFM's outstanding shares post-merger. Prior to closing, Twin Vee's existing assets and liabilities will be contributed to a trust and distributed as contingent value rights (CVRs) to current shareholders. The merger requires stockholder approval from both companies and other customary conditions, with a termination fee of $1.5M payable by Twin Vee under certain circumstances.

Completed the sale of its sevasemten compound and muscular dystrophy program to Servier for $1.55 billion upfront cash and up to $1.1 billion in regulatory and commercial milestones, for total potential consideration of up to $2.65 billion. The transaction strengthens Edgewise's balance sheet and allows it to focus on its cardiovascular pipeline, including EDG-7500 for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. item.description:

Noble Mineral Exploration and Canada Nickel signed a definitive agreement to create ExploreCo, a private exploration company consolidating large-tonnage nickel projects northeast of Timmins, Ontario. Noble contributes its Mann Township claims and other assets, receiving 20% ownership; Canada Nickel provides $5M initial funding and owns 80%. The transaction is subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval.

The company was formerly known as Ring of Fire Resources Inc. and changed its name to Noble Mineral Exploration Inc. in March 2012. The company is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.

Through its 92%-owned India subsidiary, signed a definitive agreement to acquire Riverside International Holdings Ltd, a European MGA specializing in short-term car rental insurance. Total consideration is up to £12 million (~$15 million), with £6 million payable at closing and £6 million in a three-year earn-out tied to performance milestones. To fund the acquisition, the company is conducting a bought deal offering of 20 million common shares at US$35.60 per share, raising $712 million ($818.8 million if over-allotment is exercised). The offering and acquisition are not conditional on each other; the offering is expected to close on July 14, 2026, while the acquisition closing depends on the sellers' exercise of the put option after French employee consultation procedures.

About: MDA Space Ltd. provides space technology solutions and services in Canada, the United States, Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and internationally. It offers satellite communications solutions, such as space-based broadband Internet, direct-to-device satellite communication, and IoT connectivity; MDA AURORA, a software-defined digital satellite product line; and autonomous robotics systems and services used to operate in space and on the surfaces of the Moon and Mars.

  • Counterparty: C3 Holding (CLS)
  • Stake: 70%
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-07-08
  • Expected Close: Offering: July 14, 2026; acquisition TBD
  • Next catalyst: Put option exercise by sellers after French employee consultation

item.description:

Completed the sale of the 357-room Marriott Fremont Silicon Valley hotel to SRE Acquisitions V, LLC for $53.0 million in cash, netting approximately $52.2 million after selling expenses. The sale closed on July 1, 2026, and the company continues to trade on the NYSE.

About: Ashford Hospitality Trust, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, is a real estate investment trust.
